Happy Halloween: Smiley’s Dog Costumes!
Our first event came unexpectedly, the Bridge and Tunnel Brewery was just five minute walk from our apartment but had costume!
quickly cut out avocado out Dollar Tree cardboard paper and was success!
Smiley won some toys and got growler for our fun last-minute costume.
You may have noticed the dog pictures Instagram, read the two posts wrote about our rescue Smiley you may have even read the viral Dodo article about his rescue story!
Our unexpected adoption (it only took two weeks fostering realize that Smiley belonged with forever) changed all our plans.
All those crazy Halloween parties wanted turned into going dog-friendly pub events, the dog parade and tonight we’ll going the big NYC Halloween Parade with Smiley tow.
ended going another last minute event, the Tompkins Square dog parade, Smiley wore his same avocadog costume and got outshined popes and other crazy intricate creative costumes!
was lot fun and we’re definitely going again next year with better costume.
Although most dogs wore hot dogs, delivery costumes and they rocked them, crafter got inspired something little more creative!
Tonight, the parade mainly for people but Smiley will hopefully steal the show long actually wears the costume made him and doesn’t fall apart.
Stay tuned for photos tonights shenanigans.

Two days ago sent $<hide> adoption fee Pound Hounds Res-Q, the place that pulled Smiley from Brooklyn’s high kill shelter, NYACC.
had originally planned fostering Smiley, the cute year old pit mix who was abandoned because his former owners got pregnant.
were position get dog don’t make enough money and live small apartment, but couldn’t let Smiley die.
ended adopting Smiley after just two weeks fostering, mostly because fell love with our foster boy and also because Pound Hounds Res-Q.
addition the fee, also gave small donation because everything the rescue has done for us.
Donating rescues helps them save more dogs encourage everyone the same!
The amazing thing about fostering, that it’s practically free and saves lives!
Shelters such the NYACC become overcrowded and put animals the kill list very quickly.
They barely get chance get adopted!
you foster dog, rescue will cover the vet bills and any necessary training.
Then you help the dog get decompressed, preferably crate trained and then you help them find new forever home.
Fostering great way help animals without making lifetime commitment, that many people can’t do.
hadn’t ended adopting Smiley, would have likely kept fostering, because has been rewarding.
Let tell you bit about it!
Smiley went from the NYACC the vet get neutered, then special service was hired bring him us.
had never met him even see him real life!
Smiley was friendly and curious but wouldn’t look make eye contact.
also didn’t respond commands, although were told that knew sit, stay and come.
When brought him inside, explored the place, sniffing every corner and demanding know what was behind every door.
took over hour get him slow down and rest his doggy bed.
lay there for while until tried put his harness for walk.
wouldn’t let put I’m and bared his teeth, backed off and let him sleep.
few hours later, after husband came home, let put the harness without any fuss.
his first walk with us, pulled like crazy, giving rope burn.
was strong and wouldn’t listen all outside.
immediately ordered front pulling harness, but got dragged around painfully for three whole days.
also watched some videos how get dogs attention walks and tried implement them, which only half worked.
The first evening while watched TV, saw him watching from his bed.
was the first time looked us.
Not surprising after being handed off from one person another for ten days.
was scared and confused.
crated him that first night instructed the rescue.
People want adopt crate trained dogs and had try our best although didn’t like the idea him being small cage.
barked bit but quickly went sleep.
The next morning woke wagging his tail us, was progress!
The second day acted like spoiled child, pushing his boundaries.
would jump the sofa, demand treats and pulled even harder outside.
may have had one two breakdowns that day because couldn’t connect with him.
couldn’t see anyone adopting dog that was this crazy and also didn’t know how long could spend with him, but didn’t want disappoint him like humans have the past.
That night barked more his crate night, and was stressed knowing that would have leave him alone for six hours the next day.
Everyone reassured that would okay, long wore cone was recently neutered and could rip his stitches.
When came home after our trip, that couldn’t cancel although had wanted to, found his cone out shape and was practically hanging because piece got stuck his crate.
was around his neck tight that coughed when cut off.
seemed fine otherwise and extra friendly, but was traumatized the experience.
was too scared putting him back the crate while had his cone, let him sleep our bedroom his bed.
When woke that Sunday (we had gotten him the afternoon Thursday) was completely different dog.
was calm home, looked us, asked for pats and actually listened when gave him commands.
That day took him beer festival because didn’t want leave him home alone.
had been told that shouldn’t around other dogs kids were extra careful.
But was calm around kids, accepting treats gently and wagged his tail when saw other dogs.
let him sniff few and seemed fine, were starting doubt everything that the kill shelter had said about him.
found quiet spot the chaotic festival and sat with us, observing.
People came over meet him and was friendly and loving everyone.
one believed that had just rescued him.
The next day had received front clipping harness and the moment put him, stopped pulling walks.
still got distracted outside but was much better walking calmly.
let him sniff more dogs and discovered that was super friendly but couldn’t tell between dogs that wanted play fight.
The following week started feel home.
let him sit with the couch but still kept him out bed.
behaved better every day.
Except the one time jumped the bed when screamed because saw spider but that’s because screamed and assume that was trying protect me.
During the week also let him play with some dogs while leashed.
seemed get along with everyone, ignoring the dogs who were aggressive and backed away when angry cat jumped out him.
was clearly good gentle boy!
That second weekend had him, took him amazing day care Petbuddy Services for trial day.
were nervous see how would get long with other dogs but went really well!
After day playing with dogs, was even calmer walks and less jumpy when met new dogs.
The day spent doggy daycare went out, but came home before picking Smiley up.
Our home felt empty without him even though had only been there for ten days!
That’s when decided that we’d keeping him.
But wanted wait case there were any issues with our landlord, although those were unlikely.
Wednesday took him the vet again, and even though the rescue knew were <hide>% likely adopt him, Pound Hounds Res-Q paid his bill.
Later that evening, officially paid his adoption fee and became part our family.
The next day, let him play off leash with bunch friendly dogs the park and did well, people didn’t believe that was new rescue.
Since getting Smiley there has been more stress life need figure out where will while we’re holiday.
need worry about him getting sick, hurting himself feeling lonely when he’s alone home.
also have companion home now, smiling face that makes happier and can’t walk him down the street without him getting compliments left and right.
Oh, also gets out the house more and breathing that fresh New York air!
I’ve even met few local dog owners that are quickly becoming friends.
course I’m already worrying about Smiley dying one day, but that’s just how brain works.
has already made life much better and more worthwhile.
much think that everyone should adopt dog, five, immediately, understand that not everyone int position so.
Foster!
you can’t adopt.
Donate rescues, you want help but can’t foster.
Dogs, and animals general, bring much happiness and unconditional love our hectic lives.
They deserve our help and love, they should all feel safe and happy the same way they make feel.
Please consider donating Pound Hounds Res-Q today, without them wouldn’t have this beautiful dog part our family!
We’ve been Tonsai twice now once December and the second time July.
December was hot, dry, lively and exciting!
July was dead, everything was closed, the weather was miserable!

had originally planned fostering Smiley, the cute year old pit mix who was abandoned because his former owners got pregnant.
were position get dog don’t make enough money and live small apartment, but couldn’t let Smiley die.
ended adopting Smiley after just two weeks fostering, mostly because fell love with our foster boy and also because Pound Hounds Res-Q.
addition the fee, also gave small donation because everything the rescue has done for us.

you foster dog, rescue will cover the vet bills and any necessary training.

hadn’t ended adopting Smiley, would have likely kept fostering, because has been rewarding.
Let tell you bit about it!
Smiley went from the NYACC the vet get neutered, then special service was hired bring him us.
had never met him even see him real life!
Smiley was friendly and curious but wouldn’t look make eye contact.
also didn’t respond commands, although were told that knew sit, stay and come.
When brought him inside, explored the place, sniffing every corner and demanding know what was behind every door.
took over hour get him slow down and rest his doggy bed.
lay there for while until tried put his harness for walk.
wouldn’t let put I’m and bared his teeth, backed off and let him sleep.
few hours later, after husband came home, let put the harness without any fuss.
his first walk with us, pulled like crazy, giving rope burn.
was strong and wouldn’t listen all outside.
immediately ordered front pulling harness, but got dragged around painfully for three whole days.
also watched some videos how get dogs attention walks and tried implement them, which only half worked.
The first evening while watched TV, saw him watching from his bed.
was the first time looked us.
Not surprising after being handed off from one person another for ten days.
was scared and confused.
crated him that first night instructed the rescue.
People want adopt crate trained dogs and had try our best although didn’t like the idea him being small cage.
barked bit but quickly went sleep.
The next morning woke wagging his tail us, was progress!
The second day acted like spoiled child, pushing his boundaries.
would jump the sofa, demand treats and pulled even harder outside.
may have had one two breakdowns that day because couldn’t connect with him.
couldn’t see anyone adopting dog that was this crazy and also didn’t know how long could spend with him, but didn’t want disappoint him like humans have the past.
That night barked more his crate night, and was stressed knowing that would have leave him alone for six hours the next day.
Everyone reassured that would okay, long wore cone was recently neutered and could rip his stitches.

was around his neck tight that coughed when cut off.
seemed fine otherwise and extra friendly, but was traumatized the experience.
was too scared putting him back the crate while had his cone, let him sleep our bedroom his bed.
When woke that Sunday (we had gotten him the afternoon Thursday) was completely different dog.
was calm home, looked us, asked for pats and actually listened when gave him commands.
That day took him beer festival because didn’t want leave him home alone.
had been told that shouldn’t around other dogs kids were extra careful.

let him sniff few and seemed fine, were starting doubt everything that the kill shelter had said about him.
found quiet spot the chaotic festival and sat with us, observing.
People came over meet him and was friendly and loving everyone.
one believed that had just rescued him.
The next day had received front clipping harness and the moment put him, stopped pulling walks.
still got distracted outside but was much better walking calmly.
let him sniff more dogs and discovered that was super friendly but couldn’t tell between dogs that wanted play fight.
The following week started feel home.
let him sit with the couch but still kept him out bed.
behaved better every day.
Except the one time jumped the bed when screamed because saw spider but that’s because screamed and assume that was trying protect me.
During the week also let him play with some dogs while leashed.
seemed get along with everyone, ignoring the dogs who were aggressive and backed away when angry cat jumped out him.
was clearly good gentle boy!
That second weekend had him, took him amazing day care Petbuddy Services for trial day.
were nervous see how would get long with other dogs but went really well!
After day playing with dogs, was even calmer walks and less jumpy when met new dogs.
The day spent doggy daycare went out, but came home before picking Smiley up.
Our home felt empty without him even though had only been there for ten days!
That’s when decided that we’d keeping him.

need worry about him getting sick, hurting himself feeling lonely when he’s alone home.
also have companion home now, smiling face that makes happier and can’t walk him down the street without him getting compliments left and right.

I’ve even met few local dog owners that are quickly becoming friends.
course I’m already worrying about Smiley dying one day, but that’s just how brain works.
has already made life much better and more worthwhile.
much think that everyone should adopt dog, five, immediately, understand that not everyone int position so.

you can’t adopt.
